legacy_rule_meaning: Source recognition must route the correct monthly workbook into annuity performance processing with preserved row anchors.
proof_or_equivalence_note: Phase 1 parity depends on source recognition remaining explicit and reviewable; changing the routing contract would create block-level drift.

legacy_rule_meaning: Workbook rows must normalize into the same canonical annuity performance fact meaning after governed cleansing.
proof_or_equivalence_note: Legacy cleansing behavior is preserved as an explicit capability contract; semantic output drift here is a block condition.

legacy_rule_meaning: Company identity resolution must preserve legacy business semantics even when the implementation becomes cache-first and replay-safe.
proof_or_equivalence_note: The legacy rule is retained by equivalence rather than by structure: cache hits, fallback levels, and evidence must still explain the same downstream identity decisions.

legacy_rule_meaning: Reference derivation must emit reviewable candidate payloads before publication instead of hiding the behavior inside a shared generic side effect.
proof_or_equivalence_note: Equivalent behavior is acceptable because the rebuild makes candidate generation explicit and traceable while preserving the same business intent.

legacy_rule_meaning: Publication and projection refresh must preserve the monthly contract-state and snapshot consequences that legacy operators expect.
proof_or_equivalence_note: The hook chain is replaced by explicit orchestration and projection services; parity proof comes from the same monthly business outcome, not from retaining hook-shaped control flow.

legacy_rule_meaning: Replay mismatches must produce attributable evidence and a human-reviewable compatibility case rather than informal notes.
proof_or_equivalence_note: Phase 1 accepts an explicit adjudication case flow as equivalent proof because it makes the legacy review expectation queryable and attributable.

legacy_rule_meaning: Implicit post-ETL hook ownership of snapshot refresh sequencing may retire once explicit replay orchestration proves the same business consequence and evidence chain.
proof_or_equivalence_note: Retirement is allowed only because the architecture blueprint and replay slice already replace hidden hook ownership with explicit publication and projection boundaries.

legacy_rule_meaning: Multi-sheet annual award intake and event normalization must remain visible so downstream status logic consumes the same award facts.
proof_or_equivalence_note: Phase 1 registers this breadth rule now because annual_award stays a required cross-domain fixture for annuity performance projections.

legacy_rule_meaning: Conditional plan-code enrichment may move to an explicit service boundary if the resulting award fact semantics stay equivalent.
proof_or_equivalence_note: Breadth coverage is sufficient in Phase 1: the chain is preserved conceptually, while deeper sub-stage proof is deferred to later executable slices.

legacy_rule_meaning: Multi-sheet annual loss intake and event normalization must preserve the reported-loss fact shape consumed by downstream projections.
proof_or_equivalence_note: Phase 1 keeps this breadth rule explicit because annual_loss remains part of the accepted first-wave coverage set.

legacy_rule_meaning: Conditional plan-code and identity enrichment may be rebuilt with a clearer service boundary if final loss fact semantics remain equivalent.
proof_or_equivalence_note: The breadth row records the parity-sensitive enrichment chain without pretending Phase 1 already deep-mapped each annual_loss sub-stage.
